statement.p: Currently, an artist can sell 220 paintings every year at the price of {\$110.00} per painting. Each time he raises the price per painting by {\$10.00}, he sells 10 fewer paintings every year.

Assume he will raise the price per painting x times, then he will sell 220-10x paintings every year at the price of 110+10x dollars. His yearly income can be modeled by the equation: i=(110+10x)(220-10x) where i stands for his yearly income in dollars. If the artist wants to earn {\$25{,}200.00} per year from selling paintings, what new price should he set?
To earn {\$25{,}200.00} per year, the artist could sell his paintings at two different prices. The lower price is per painting, and the higher price is per painting.

solution.p: If the artist wants to make {\$25{,}200.00} per year, the equation becomes:
We will change the equation to standard form:
Next, we can solve this quadratic equation with the Quadratic Formula. Identify that a=-100,b=1100,c=-1000.
If the artist raises the price 1 times, the new price would be 110+10\cdot1=120 dollars per painting.
If the artist raises the price 10 times, the new price would be 110+10\cdot10=210 dollars per painting.
To earn {\$25{,}200.00} per year, the artist could sell his paintings at two different prices. The lower price is {\$120.00} per painting, and the higher price is {\$210.00} dollars per painting.
